subject: Chemistry
title: Photochromic Lenses: Color on Demand
description: A fast breakdown of Photochromic Lenses: Color on Demand for builders.

hook.text: Transition lenses darken in sunlight and clear indoors. The molecules inside them change shape when hit by UV light.

buildup.text: Silver chloride crystals in the glass absorb UV photons. The energy breaks Ag-Cl bonds, freeing silver atoms that darken the lens.

discovery.text: Remove the UV and the reaction reverses—silver recombines with chlorine and the lens clears. It's a reversible photoreaction.

twist.text: Modern versions use organic molecules that switch between open (colored) and closed (clear) forms. No silver needed.

climax.text: Smart windows use similar chemistry. Electrochromic glass dims on command, cutting building energy use by 25%.

punchline.text: Molecules that reshape under light. Chemistry creates color you can switch on and off.

quiz.question: How do traditional photochromic lenses darken in sunlight?
options: UV light breaks silver chloride bonds, freeing silver atoms that absorb visible light
Heat from sunlight expands dark pigment molecules in the lens
A thin film of liquid crystal aligns to block light
